What does Erica actually provide for a team? I read her kit a couple of times but I just can't figure out what makes her worth using over any other character XD
A bit of all
Damage, str, shield, weakness, vulnerable

She's kind of hard to understand for me, too. Erica has a relatively low arithmetica impact for a keyflare character, since her keyflare card is free and generates a lot of temporary strength or alert, whose strikes and defenses aren't abject dogshit garbage on account of her base stats, and that incidentally helps her get her exalt out more often.
Her X cost used to work with her rouse to just dump arithmetica if your hand was bad, but Ultra generally doesn't have the problem of "there's nothing here I want to play"
and values got super inflated from early on so the X cost has a bit of a limited reach too
People will tell me that she's a strong defender character but I've never seen a comp that relies on her being in it

Yeah, maybe I'll take another look at her with a different mindset. I didn't level her so a lot of the numbers on her cards are just kind of useless. Like, when enemies are trying to hit you for like 1800 damage a turn "a little shield" doesn't seem like it matters much XD
Do her enlightenments provide a lot for her too? Working on Aigis and her WoD rn so I don't think I could spare the currency anyway but eventually I'll get to it
She also gives you 21% crit rate/damage

I think she works well with characters who have a lot of smaller shield sources on account of the alert scaling she provides with Retract Mecha and/or her Rouse, and she removes Weakness or Fragile too
Just a lot of little role compression. I think she goes well with Castor, for example.
Not unlike Horla, but with a fat brick of stats instead of a 1 cost Vuln/Weak
Makes sense, I have Castor e0 but I haven't levelled him either since I didn't need him to hit 350 on dtide. Gonna look at all the stuff I have and see if I can push a bit further next reset

honestly, one of the best things about her is that low impact; she doesn't cost a lot of arithmetica, doesn't demand a lot of your hand size, and just so happens to be a unit you can +12 without spending pulls on

Unlike all of the other status conditions that you put on the boss, Corrosion isn't affected by damage resistance, so as long as the characters you have do enough damage to trigger all of the Corrosion
*and Final Verdict*
Corrosion is quite a cheese tho, and can bypass fortress + barrier
but u have to apply it every turn
Fortress affects the damage that triggers Corrosion, but it basically multiplies your damage back into being normal again
This still has a bad impact on GHelot's damage since it's predicated on triggering Bleeds, and she won't be accumulating Bleed properly, but it'll all work back into the Corrosion as expected

You're having this problem right now for two reasons
1. When Thais clones Bloodthirsty Flail, they're at 3 cost and not 2, so you can't easily play more than one Flail; E2 Thais is a big breakpoint
2. You don't have Lone Seed, so you can't make copies of Flail that will sit in your hand when you need them

E2 Catena is also a big breakpoint because that should be when Flail gets Prepare 2, so the cost goes down to 2 the turn after you make it, then Thais exalts to make two more copies of 2 cost Flail, and then you consume a Scion of Purity with GHelot's Devour and the cost becomes 0 for all of them
but that doesn't matter for this scenario specifically because Catena doesn't actually hit Hydra that hard because the rear snake is unhittable

E3 is really good. E2 is just so much bigger than that.

hi, since no one answered you, Discard comp mainly consists of units that are reliant on constant deck refresh.
